tasks.title: Verify and align sanity check command triggers with user expectations. Sanity-check the sanity-check implementation to avoid wasted time and resources
evidence: User request: Sanity checks should run automatically on ralph run, ralph doctor, ralph task, ralph queue commands
User request: Sanity checks should run ONCE at startup, not repeatedly during ralph run loop
User request: Sanity checks should NOT run during queue validation within the loop
Current implementation: sanity checks run in main.rs before command dispatch via should_run_sanity_checks()
Current implementation: run_loop in commands/run/mod.rs does NOT call sanity checks again
Current behavior: sanity checks only trigger for Command::Run and Command::Queue(Validate)
Gap: ralph task commands do not currently trigger sanity checks
Gap: docs/cli.md may need updating to document which commands trigger sanity checks
plan: Verify current sanity check behavior runs only once per ralph invocation (confirmed in main.rs)
Verify run_loop does not re-trigger sanity checks during task iteration (confirmed in commands/run/mod.rs)
Review should_run_sanity_checks() to add ralph task commands if not present
Review should_run_sanity_checks() to add all ralph queue commands (not just validate)
Test ralph run loop --max-tasks 5 to confirm sanity check only appears once
Update docs/cli.md to document which commands trigger automatic sanity checks
Run make ci to verify all tests pass
notes: Current implementation already satisfies 'run once per session' requirement
Sanity checks run in main.rs:59-74 before command routing, not during loop execution
The loop in run_loop() calls run_one() repeatedly but sanity checks are not in that path
If implementation is already correct, task is to verify and document only
tasks.request: Read the plan at this file .ralph/cache/plans/RQ-0454.md. Under key design decisions, number four, it says run automatically on Ralph's startup before command execution, and I agree with that. I want that. That is the intended behavior I want. Like, if someone runs Ralph, whether they run any command for the first time, like they open a command prompt in a project and they run Ralph. With the exception of Ralph in it to initialize. If they run Ralph run, for example, it should do the automatic check. If they run Ralph run one, it should do the automatic check. I want to make sure that it doesn't rerun that check again and again and again in the same session though. So it does it once at startup. But as the loop continues, it shouldn't be rechecking during the loop. And it shouldn't be checking during the queue validation. It should really only be run like the execution of the checks should really only be run when it's used with 'ralph run' 'ralph doctor' 'ralph task' 'ralph queue'. What I dont want to happen is I run 'ralph run loop --max-tasks 5' and see the validation snanity check happening several times. It should do the sanity check once initially and then not again until 'ralph run loop --max-tasks5' terminates and the user invokes another 'ralph' command. Otherwise it just adds meaningless overhead. Maybe this is how it is already implemented, or there's no reason for concern due to how the workflows and code works, but I wanted to call this out and have a task built to have this clarified, and fixed if it doesnt align with my expectations. If the current implementation is valid then no changes are required.

tasks.title: Fix Kimi runner integration - CLI command structure is incorrect
evidence: Kimi runner fails with exit code 2 - the agent exits immediately
Current implementation tries to run: kimi --model <model> --output-format stream-json with stdin payload
Kimi CLI requires --print flag for non-interactive mode (per kimi --help: 'Note: print mode implicitly adds --yolo')
Kimi CLI structure is: kimi [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]... - no 'run' or 'exec' subcommand like other runners
Kimi expects prompts via --prompt/-p flag or stdin with --print mode
Print mode docs: https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/customization/print-mode.html
Wire mode docs: https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/customization/wire-mode.html
User config: 'runner': 'kimi', 'model': 'kimi-code/kimi-for-coding' (from ~/.kimi/config.toml)
AGENT DECISION NEEDED: Compare wire mode vs print mode for Ralph's use case - wire mode may be more appropriate than print mode
plan: Run scripts/runner_cli_inventory.sh to capture full kimi CLI help and verify command structure
Read kimi print-mode documentation at https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/customization/print-mode.html
Read kimi wire-mode documentation at https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/customization/wire-mode.html
DECIDE: Compare wire mode vs print mode for Ralph's runner integration - wire mode may provide better streaming/tool support
Analyze current run_kimi() implementation in runners.rs - it builds: kimi --model <m> --output-format stream-json (no --print or --wire flag)
Fix run_kimi() to use correct CLI based on chosen mode: kimi --print|--wire --output-format stream-json --model <model> --prompt <prompt>
Update cli_spec.rs apply_kimi_options() if needed for --print/--wire flag handling
Test the fix by running a simple task with kimi runner
Update runner_cli_inventory.sh if kimi has unique patterns to capture
notes: Kimi CLI has commands: login, logout, term, acp, info, mcp, web - but NO run/exec command
The --print flag is a global option, not a subcommand
Compare with working runners: codex uses 'exec', opencode uses 'run', gemini/claude use direct execution
WIRE MODE vs PRINT MODE: Wire mode may be better for Ralph's streaming/tool-use needs - agent should evaluate both
tasks.request: Investigate the kimi runner integration. It does not work. 'kimi' is available via the CLI e.g. 'kimi --help'. Check the outputput of the scripts/runner_cli_inventory.sh script. Check the docs at https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/customization/print-mode.html and https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/customization/. I tried using kimi as the runner in a different project and it failed. config.json had "runner": "kimi", "model": "kimi-code/kimi-for-coding", which is what the default kimi config.toml file at '/Users/mitchfultz/.kimi/config.toml' shows.

tasks.title: Implement task time tracking with estimates vs actual duration
evidence: Task has created_at and completed_at but no estimated_duration field

reports.rs has burndown and stats but no velocity or estimation accuracy metrics
plan: Add `estimated_duration` field to Task (minutes or hours)
Add `time_spent` field to track actual duration
Add `ralph task estimate <TASK_ID> <DURATION>` command
Auto-track time when task moves to doing status
Add `ralph queue stats --estimation-accuracy` report
Update burndown chart to show estimated vs actual completion rate
Add tests for time tracking calculations

tasks.title: Add task relationships beyond dependencies (blocks, relates_to, duplicates)

plan: Add `blocks` field to Task (array of task IDs this task blocks)
Add `relates_to` field for loose coupling between related tasks
Add `duplicates` field for marking tasks as duplicates of others
Add `ralph task relate <TASK_ID> <RELATION> <OTHER_TASK_ID>` command
Update queue graph to show different relationship types with different edge styles
Add validation to prevent circular blocking relationships
Add tests for relationship validation and graph rendering

Update parent task progress based on subtask completion percentage
Block parent task completion until all subtasks are done
Update TUI to show collapsible subtask hierarchies
Update queue graph to show parent-child relationships
Add tests for subtask lifecycle and parent completion blocking
